step_by_step_thinking: \boxed{A} Based on the evidence, endothelin-1 (ET-1) plays a physiological role in regulating vascular tone and sodium/water handling via actions on endothelial and renal tissues. During hemodialysis, intradialytic vascular resistance surges are linked to acute alterations in endothelial cell function — consistent with dysregulation of ET-1 signaling given its established role as a potent vasoconstrictor whose activity rises under conditions of volume overload and shear stress. Furthermore, studies show that blocking ET receptors improves blood pressure and reduces hypertension markers, suggesting relevance to hemodynamics observed during dialysis.
raw_response:
\boxed{A} Based on the evidence, endothelin-1 (ET-1) plays a physiological role in regulating vascular tone and sodium/water handling via actions on endothelial and renal tissues. During hemodialysis, intradialytic vascular resistance surges are linked to acute alterations in endothelial cell function — consistent with dysregulation of ET-1 signaling given its established role as a potent vasoconstrictor whose activity rises under conditions of volume overload and shear stress. Furthermore, studies show that blocking ET receptors improves blood pressure and reduces hypertension markers, suggesting relevance to hemodynamics observed during dialysis.
\boxed{A}
